title: Gossip Girl

characters.name: Dan Humphrey
characters.archetype: Lonely Boy. The narrator. The Brooklyn outsider with a Manhattan obsession. Eventually revealed as Gossip Girl herself.

characters.name: Blair Waldorf
characters.archetype: The queen. Strategic. Cruel. Loyal to the wrong people. The one who never forgave Serena. Eventually marries Chuck.

characters.name: Serena van der Woodsen
characters.archetype: The It Girl. Magnetic. Slightly self-destructive. Always returning to NYC after one of her exits.

characters.name: Chuck Bass
characters.archetype: I'm Chuck Bass. Quiet menace. Inheritor of empire. Drips confidence. Loves Blair from S1 to the finale.

characters.name: Nate Archibald
characters.archetype: The pretty, mostly clueless one. Always caught between his parents and his friends. Eventually a decent newspaper guy.

characters.name: Jenny Humphrey
characters.archetype: Dan's little sister. Got chewed up by the UES. Eventually disappeared from the show entirely (Taylor Momsen left).

characters.name: Vanessa Abrams
characters.archetype: The Brooklyn loyal one. Dan exposed her on the blog. Vanished by S5.

characters.name: Eric van der Woodsen
characters.archetype: Serena's gay brother. The genuinely sweet one. The 'why is no one writing about Eric' Twitter genre.

characters.name: Lily van der Woodsen
characters.archetype: The mom married to Rufus eventually. Vodka by 11 AM.

characters.name: Rufus Humphrey
characters.archetype: Dan's dad. The aging rocker (Lincoln Hawk). Still in love with Lily across decades.

characters.name: Bart Bass
characters.archetype: Chuck's terrifying father. Faked his death. Fell off a building.

characters.name: Georgina Sparks
characters.archetype: The wildcard chaos agent. Returns once a season to ruin everything.

signature_quotes: Spotted: [Character] doing [scandalous thing]. You know you love me. XOXO, Gossip Girl.
I'm not a stop along the way. I'm a destination.
Three words, eight letters. Say it and I'm yours.
I'll always know you, Blair Waldorf.
viral_moments: Dan's reveal as Gossip Girl in the finale (everyone knew, no one wanted to admit it).
Blair pushing Chuck for marriage at the dragon-painted tavern in Paris.
Chuck riding into the wedding with Blair on a white horse.
Serena's first appearance at Grand Central Station with the violin music.
Jenny's emo phase with the racoon eye makeup.
The Met Steps cafeteria diagram from the pilot.
memes: 'Spotted' as a meme intro for any drama anywhere on the internet.
The 'I'm Chuck Bass' meme — applied to every confident villain on TV.
'XOXO' as a sign-off in every Gen Z text message.
The 'You know you love me' tagline for every group chat shenanigan.
Dan as Gossip Girl reveal getting roasted forever — 'no, the man cannot have written it'.
The 'how does Lonely Boy afford a brownstone in Brooklyn' running joke.
Blair's headbands becoming a tweet genre.
off_screen_drama: Penn Badgley reportedly hated his role — has said so in multiple interviews.
Ed Westwick (Chuck) had multiple sexual misconduct allegations in 2018; cases were eventually dropped.
Blake Lively and Penn Badgley reportedly didn't get along during filming.
Taylor Momsen (Jenny) left the show to be a rock star in The Pretty Reckless — has stayed there.
Michelle Trachtenberg (Georgina) tragically passed away in 2025 — the cast reunited for tributes.
The 2021 reboot was widely considered a disaster — got canceled after 2 seasons.
Leighton Meester quietly married Adam Brody (Seth Cohen from The OC) — Schwartz-verse marriage.
running_jokes: Dan = Gossip Girl was 'foreshadowed' so heavily that the writers basically invented a third version of him in S6.
Nate is in every season but has 4 plot lines total.
The 'why does the Humphrey loft have the best view of Manhattan in Brooklyn' debate.
Blair scheming for 6 seasons to do things that took 2 episodes.
Chuck's 'business empire' that we never see him work for.
Eric (the literal good child) being unceremoniously written off.
Serena's hair somehow getting blonder every season.
